title: Afraid? Of Whom Am I Afraid?
content:
Afraid? Of whom am I afraid?
    Not death; for who is he?
    The porter of my father's lodge
    As much abasheth me.

    Of life? 'T were odd I fear a thing
    That comprehendeth me
    In one or more existences
    At Deity's decree.

    Of resurrection? Is the east
    Afraid to trust the morn
    With her fastidious forehead?
    As soon impeach my crown!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emily Elizabeth Dickinson, titled "Afraid? Of Whom Am I Afraid?", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
